community.label: The Vibe
community.heading: More Than a Kite School
community.description: Máncora Kite Club is a crew. Every session, every downwinder, every sunset — we share the stoke with riders from all over the world. Whether you are just starting or chasing your first wave, you will find a home here.

story.label: Our Story
story.headline: 15 years reading Mancora's water.
story.paragraph1: I am Enrique, founder of Mancora Kite Club. I have spent more than 15 years living, riding and reading this coastline: wind, swell, current and the small details that make northern Peru special.
story.paragraph2: Our work is simple: safe coaching, real progression and an ocean experience that feels personal. More than a school, it is a way to understand the sea and want to come back to it.

intro.title: Learn Kitesurf in The Club
intro.text: The first time someone was seen practicing wingfoil was in 2019, when Robby Naish appeared in Hawaii on a board with a foil and handling a wing. Those images caused so much emotion that in a few months we began to see videos and photos on all social networks of different types of wings and people practicing this strange sport. It did not take long before this sport began to rise, with countless sails appearing across beaches and lakes. Without a doubt, it is a sport that will leave many fans.

3.text: I loved learning kite here at the best school in the north.

1.text: Very good school, great atmosphere, and Mancora is a wonderful place where I was lucky enough to ride alongside whales and dolphins... what more could you want? 😅😅

6.text: Very good experience at Mancora Kite Club. Kike makes you feel comfortable and motivated; he has a lot of experience and total command of the sport. Totally recommended!

2.text: Great instructors and even better people! I went with my partner and she took classes; she loved it. We will definitely come back to share and, of course, ride!

7.text: Thank you Kike for giving me the best experience at the school! So much patience and dedication. Perfect attention.

8.text: I spent a week practicing Kitesurf and had no trouble thanks to the school team. We visited Máncora's main spot, and on low-wind days we went looking for wind, so we still got to ride. The school team is kind and friendly.

4.text: We traveled as a family and my teenage kids took kitesurf lessons at Mancora Kite Club. We were fascinated! The team was super kind, patient, and motivating with the kids. The weather is ideal, and the club atmosphere is safe and welcoming. We also joined a family kite trip that was the most fun part of the journey. We left with unforgettable memories. Many thanks to the team for their dedication and good energy.

5.text: A very professional Kite school in northern Peru. They know how to take you to the best Kite and Kitesurf spots. Ask for Kike 🙌🏼🤙🏼🤙🏼

Wingfoil-vs-Kitesurf-Choosing-Your-Weapo.title: Wingfoil vs Kitesurf: Choosing Your Weapon

q1.q: Can I learn kitesurfing if I've never done water sports?
q1.a: Absolutely! You don't need any prior water sports experience. Our IKO certified instructors will teach you everything from scratch, starting with kite control on the beach before entering the water.

q2.q: How many hours do I need to start?
q2.a: Most beginners need about 9 to 12 hours to become independent. We recommend our 3-day (6 hours) or 5-day (10+ hours) beginner courses to build a solid foundation and start riding the board.

q3.q: Is it dangerous to learn kitesurfing?
q3.a: With modern equipment and professional instruction, kitesurfing is very safe. We use the latest safety gear, teach in safe conditions, and our instructors are always by your side.

q4.q: What happens if I am afraid or don't progress fast?
q4.a: It's completely normal to feel nervous! Our instructors adapt to your pace. If you need more time on kite control before entering the water, we will take it step by step until you feel confident.

q5.q: What is the difference between kitesurf and wingfoil?
q5.a: Kitesurfing uses a large kite attached to a harness to pull you on a twin-tip or surfboard. Wingfoiling uses a handheld wing (not attached to your body) and a board with a hydrofoil that lifts you above the water. Both are incredibly fun, but kitesurfing is usually easier to learn initially.

q6.q: Do I need to bring my own equipment?
q6.a: No, you don't need to bring anything. All our classes include premium equipment (kite, board, harness, impact vest, helmet, and wetsuit). Just bring swimwear, sunscreen, and a good vibe!

q7.q: What course do you recommend for beginners?
q7.a: We recommend the Beginner (3 days) or Full Progression (5 days) courses. These give you enough time to learn kite control, body dragging, and water starts on the board.

q8.q: Can I take classes as a couple?
q8.a: Yes! Our Semi-Private classes are perfect for couples or friends. You will share one instructor and one kite between two students, taking turns and learning from each other's progress.

q9.a: We teach students from 12 years old, provided they weigh at least 40kg (88 lbs). For younger children, please contact us to evaluate their specific case.

q10.a: Máncora is blessed with consistent, smooth thermal winds almost all year round, typically between 15 to 25 knots. These are ideal conditions for learning safely and progressing quickly.

q11.a: Semi-private means that one instructor teaches two students who share one set of equipment. It's a great way to rest between attempts and learn by watching your partner. Prices are per person.

q12.q: Can I combine classes with accommodation and rentals?
q12.a: Yes! You can use our Custom Trip Builder to create your ideal package, mixing kitesurf classes, gear rental, and a stay at our beachfront Bali Lodge. Build your trip here.

q1.a: With the right equipment, certified instructors, and safety protocols, kitesurfing is a very safe sport. At Mancora Kite Club, we prioritize safety in every session.

q2.a: No. Our courses are designed for absolute beginners and advanced progression.

q3.q: Is Máncora a good place to learn?
q3.a: Yes. Máncora offers constant wind, wide waters, and ideal conditions to progress quickly.

q4.q: How long does it take to learn?
q4.a: The time it takes to learn varies depending on the individual, but most people can learn the basics in 3-5 days.

q5.q: What is the best time to learn?
q5.a: The best time to learn is during the windy season.

q1.a: Yes. All professional equipment is included: kite, harness, board, helmet, and impact vest.

hero.title: Welcome to the Wind
hero.subtitle: Discover why Máncora is the ideal place to start your kitesurfing journey.

intro.title: Easier Than It Looks
intro.text: Many people think kitesurfing requires extreme upper body strength or prior experience in extreme sports. The truth? It's all about technique and understanding the wind. With our step-by-step methodology and safe, shallow waters, anyone can learn to fly. Kitesurfing is a low-impact sport where your harness takes all the power from the kite, leaving you free to steer with just two fingers.
intro.imageAlt: Beginner student flying a kite on the beach

whyMancora.title: Why Learn in Máncora?
whyMancora.subtitle: Máncora offers unique conditions that make it one of the best spots in the world to learn kitesurfing.

wind.title: Consistent Thermal Winds
wind.desc: 15–25 knots of steady cross-onshore wind almost every day of the year. No gusts, no surprises.

water.title: Flat, Shallow Water
water.desc: Waist-deep water for hundreds of meters means you can always stand up and feel safe while learning.

weather.title: Year-Round Sunshine
weather.desc: Warm water (24°C), blue skies, and no wetsuit needed. Just boardshorts and sunscreen.

certified.title: IKO Certified School
certified.desc: Our instructors hold international IKO certifications. Your safety and progression are our top priorities.

community.title: Vibrant Kite Community
community.desc: Join a welcoming community of riders from around the world. Share sessions, stories, and sunsets.

stay.title: Stay Right at the Spot
stay.desc: Our Bali Lodge is steps from the kite beach. Wake up, grab breakfast, and walk to your lesson.

whatsIncluded.title: What's Included in Your Lesson?
whatsIncluded.subtitle: You don't need to bring anything. We provide all the premium gear and safety equipment.

items.board: Twin-tip boards for all sizes and levels
items.harness: Comfortable waist harness fitted to your body

learningPath.title: Your Progression Step by Step
learningPath.subtitle: A structured, safe, and fun way to learn from scratch.

day1.title: Day 1: Beach Control
day1.desc: Learn the theory of wind windows, safety systems, and how to control a small trainer kite on the beach before moving to a real inflatable kite. By the end of the session, you'll be flying confidently on the sand.

day2.title: Day 2: Body Dragging
day2.desc: Time to get wet! You'll learn how to use the power of the kite to pull yourself through the water without a board. This is essential for recovering your board and understanding the true power of the wind.

day3.title: Day 3: The Water Start
day3.desc: The most exciting part. You'll combine kite control with the board and attempt your first water starts. We'll focus on body position, board alignment, and managing power to get you riding your first few meters.

faq.title: Common Beginner Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you want to know before your first session.

q1.q: Can I learn if I've never done water sports?
q1.a: Absolutely! No prior experience is needed. We teach everything from scratch.

q2.q: How many hours do I need to start riding?
q2.a: Most beginners need 9–12 hours (3–5 days) to become independent on the board.

q3.a: With modern equipment and professional instruction, it is very safe. We follow strict safety protocols.

q4.q: What if I'm scared or I don't progress fast?
q4.a: Totally normal! Our instructors adapt to your pace. There's no rush—we go step by step.

q5.q: Do I need to bring my own gear?
q5.a: No. All premium equipment is included in every lesson: kite, board, harness, helmet, and impact vest.

q6.a: We teach students from 12 years old, with a minimum weight of 40 kg.

cta.description: Join us in Máncora and experience the thrill of kitesurfing with certified instructors and premium gear.

seo.title: Beginners Guide to Kitesurfing in Máncora | Mancora Kite Club
seo.description: Everything you need to know about starting kitesurfing in Máncora. Learn about the progression, equipment, and why it's easier than it looks.
